title: Auto-tag Release Workflow
description: Walkthrough of the new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically creates and pushes a Git tag when a version bump is merged to main.

steps.title: Workflow trigger and permissions
steps.description: The workflow triggers on every push to main (no path filter). Permissions are set to contents:read for the GITHUB_TOKEN (least privilege). The actual tag write uses the PAT.

steps.title: Checkout with PAT
steps.description: Uses actions/checkout@v4 with secrets.RELEASE_TOKEN (a PAT). This is critical: GITHUB_TOKEN-initiated events don't trigger downstream workflows, so the PAT makes the tag push appear as an external event that triggers publish.yml.

steps.title: Version extraction with fail-closed handling
steps.description: Reads version from package.json using jq. Uses set -euo pipefail for strict mode, plus an explicit check for empty or 'null' values. If version can't be read, the step exits non-zero (fail-closed).

steps.title: Remote tag existence check (idempotency)
steps.description: Queries the remote via git ls-remote to check if the tag already exists. This is authoritative and works with shallow clones (default fetch-depth: 1). If the tag exists, outputs exists=true and the next step is skipped.

steps.title: Conditional tag creation and push
steps.description: Only runs when the tag doesn't exist (if: steps.check-tag.outputs.exists == 'false'). Creates the tag at HEAD and pushes it to origin. The PAT credentials from checkout enable the push and ensure the tag event triggers publish.yml.
